title: Born in Simbirsk
description: Vladimir Ilyich Ulyanov is born on April 22, 1870, in Simbirsk.

title: Brother Aleksandr Executed
description: In 1887 Lenin's older brother Aleksandr is executed for plotting against Tsar Alexander III.

title: Expelled from Kazan University
description: In 1887 Lenin is expelled after taking part in a student protest at Kazan University.

title: Earns Law Degree
description: In 1891 Lenin passes his law examinations with top marks.

title: Practices Law in Samara
description: In 1892 he begins practicing law in Samara after gaining admission to the bar.

title: Moves to St. Petersburg
description: In 1893 Lenin moves to St. Petersburg and becomes active in Marxist circles.

title: Forms Union for the Struggle
description: In 1895 Lenin helps organize the Union for the Struggle for the Liberation of the Working Class.

title: Arrested by Tsarist Police
description: In December 1895 Lenin is arrested after the Union's leaders are rounded up.

title: Exiled to Siberia
description: In 1897 Lenin is sentenced to internal exile in Siberia.

title: Marries Nadezhda Krupskaya
description: Lenin marries Nadezhda Krupskaya on July 22, 1898.

title: Launches Iskra
description: In 1900 Lenin helps launch the newspaper Iskra to unite Russian Marxists.

title: Adopts the Name Lenin
description: By 1901 he is using the pseudonym Lenin in underground political work.

title: Publishes What Is to Be Done?
description: In 1902 Lenin publishes What Is to Be Done?, setting out his theory of the vanguard party.

title: Bolshevik-Menshevik Split
description: At the 1903 party congress Lenin's faction becomes known as the Bolsheviks.

title: Returns During 1905 Revolution
description: Lenin returns to Russia during the Revolution of 1905.

title: Forms Distinct Bolshevik Organization
description: In 1912 Lenin leads a separate Bolshevik organization within the Russian socialist movement.

title: Opposes World War I
description: During World War I Lenin denounces the conflict from exile in Switzerland.

title: Returns to Petrograd
description: Lenin returns to Petrograd in April 1917 after the February Revolution.

title: Presents the April Theses
description: In April 1917 Lenin calls for 'All Power to the Soviets' and rejects support for the Provisional Government.

title: Flees After the July Days
description: After the failed July Days uprising in 1917, Lenin goes into hiding in Finland.

title: October Revolution
description: On November 7, 1917, the Bolsheviks seize power in Petrograd under Lenin's leadership.

title: Signs Brest-Litovsk Peace
description: In March 1918 Lenin's government signs the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk to leave World War I.

title: Survives Assassination Attempt
description: In August 1918 Lenin is seriously wounded in an assassination attempt.

title: Founds the Comintern
description: In 1919 Lenin helps found the Communist International in Moscow.

title: Launches the New Economic Policy
description: In 1921 Lenin introduces the New Economic Policy after the crisis of War Communism.

title: USSR Formed
description: In December 1922 the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics is formally established.

title: Suffers First Major Stroke
description: In 1922 Lenin suffers the first of the strokes that remove him from active leadership.

title: Dictates Lenin's Testament
description: From late 1922 into early 1923 Lenin dictates the political document later called Lenin's Testament.

title: Dies at Gorki
description: Lenin dies on January 21, 1924, at Gorki near Moscow.
